about.p1: I'm Diana, a Portuguese photographer and filmmaker with a deep love for visual storytelling. With a degree in Digital Arts and Multimedia from ESAD, I've built a practice rooted in both technical craft and artistic curiosity, always searching for the image that says what words can't.
about.p2: Based in Porto, I work on projects locally and abroad, drawn to the kind of work that pushes creative boundaries and leaves a mark. I thrive on experimentation, adapt quickly to new environments, and bring genuine passion to every project I take on.
about.p3: If you're building something that deserves to be seen, I'd love to be part of it.

aqua.title: Aqua
aqua.p1: Aqua is a photography project focused on the sensitive observation of river water, not only as a natural element, but as a symbol of movement, passage and transformation.
aqua.p2: More than documenting a landscape, this project is a visual study of texture, light and rhythm. The photographs explore the relationship between the natural and the abstract, showing how water can take on different expressions depending on the frame, the light or the moment.
aqua.p3: The river water, always in flow, served as a metaphor for the creative process itself: constant, unpredictable, alive.

reflexo.title: Reflexo
reflexo.p1: This photographic project was developed in a university context based on a single word-theme: reflection.
reflexo.p2: From this seemingly simple starting point, I delved into a visual and conceptual exploration of the multiple layers that the term can carry, from the literal mirror to the deepest introspection.
reflexo.p3: This work reflects a sensitive and subjective exploration of the relationship between myself and the city where I grew up, Amarante. The images, characterized by a melancholic and dreamlike tone, reinforce the idea of an "impossible city", a city that is not only a geographical space, but also a reflection of my emotions and memories. This perspective gives the work a personal and enigmatic dimension, where reality and subjectivity come together.

genesis.title: Genesis
genesis.p1: This project was developed in a university context, where the proposal was to develop a photobook based on a song. Genesis, by the artist RAYE, was the chosen one.
genesis.p2: The photobook thus emerges as a collection of visual emotions, where the experimental intersects with the aesthetic, and where playing with the image was essential to achieve a result that was more sensorial and aesthetic than literal.
genesis.p3: The creative process was guided by freedom of interpretation, allowing me to explore new visual approaches and develop a bolder and more intuitive look.

pele.title: Cartography of the Skin
pele.p1: In this work, I take the skin as an intimate and symbolic territory, exploring the body in a fragmented way through black and white photography. More than an anatomical representation, each image is an isolated fragment that gains autonomy and invites sensory and emotional interpretations—internal landscapes that carry marks, memories and affections.
pele.p2: The absence of color is an intentional choice: it reduces the image to its formal essence and intensifies texture, light and shadow, revealing details that normally go unnoticed. It is in these details that I find poetry and truth.
pele.p3: "Cartography of the Skin" is an invitation to look slowly—to touch with your eyes what we so often hide from ourselves and others.

t1.title: T1 Jams
t1.p1: T1 Jams was launched as an initiative created by Eduardo Hora, Guilherme Aguiar and Joana Pinto with the aim of promoting new emerging artists from Porto. More than just a concert, it is a space for sharing and expression for lovers of music and art where, in an intimate setting, the artist and the audience are part of creating a moment in which the present takes precedence over everything else.
t1.p2: The first edition featured performances by ManWillOnly and Lopo Romão.

biennale.p1: One of my most recent professional challenges was capturing the 4th edition of Porto Design Biennale, themed Time is Present. Designing the Common — a project with a real and lasting impact on communities. This selection is a small glimpse of what the Biennale was.

munna.p1: This project involves the production of an artistic video lasting around three minutes and six short videos focusing on the brand's products for commercial purposes. We aim to explore a visual world where the classical and the dreamlike merge into a single universe, following the journey of two characters who, upon discovering the furniture, encounter a new reality, emerging into an environment where 'classics' take on a new meaning.
munna.p2: The contrast between the house's classical architecture and the new approach to the furniture's characteristic style not only highlights MUNNA's craftsmanship but also emphasises the inspiration that cultural heritage offers the brand. In this way, the videos not only promote the products but also reinforce MUNNA's identity as a link between tradition and innovation in interior design.

maiscomoeu.p1: Developed as a final undergraduate project, "Mais Como Um Eu" is a short film that was born from the desire to explore the fusion between art and personal identity. In this work, I take on the challenge of placing myself at the center of the narrative, not only as a creator, but also as an emotional protagonist, reflecting on the limits between the creative self and the intimate self.
maiscomoeu.p2: The short film proposes a visual and sensorial journey, where cinematographic language is used as a form of internal expression. Through visual metaphors, movement and a more experimental aesthetic approach, I challenged myself to translate complex emotions such as doubt, introspection and self-knowledge into images that speak for themselves.
maiscomoeu.p3: This project was, above all, an exercise in vulnerability and overcoming. I faced my own creative and emotional barriers, using cinema as a tool for self-reflection and, at the same time, for communicating with the public. "Mais Como Um Eu" represents a remarkable stage in my academic and artistic career, where the creative process was as important as the final result. A work where the personal becomes universal and where art serves as a mirror.

entrenos.p1: This short film explores the fractured relationship between two brothers, Lu and Vitor, who symbolically reconnect through their childhood home, left behind by their now-deceased parents. Although they never physically cross paths, both revisit the space, which Lu sees as a refuge, and Vitor as a place of confrontation with regret. Through an introspective narrative, the film offers a reflection on grief, emotional distance and the possibility of symbolic reconciliation, using the house as a metaphor for family ties that have been lost but remain latent.
entrenos.p2: Developed as a final degree project, 'Entre Nós' is a short film directed and produced by Joana Pinto, Maria Miguel Silva and Hien Ahn Tran. The opportunity to develop this project arose at a time of transition, when it became essential to seize every opportunity. Above all, it stemmed from the desire to begin building a meaningful path for ourselves as artists.

coppelia.p1: In a dystopian universe where emotions are illegal and freedom is a mirage, Coppélia 2.0 reinterprets classical ballet through a radically contemporary visual and sensory language. The piece merges dance, fashion, music and multimedia in a synesthetic show, where body and digital image intertwine in a cyberpunk narrative about resistance, love and emancipation.
coppelia.p2: Conceived based on Cinema 4D and After Effects, the visuals were created to dialogue with the choreography throughout the two acts of the play — more than 24 scenes — exploring contrasts between classical and futuristic, hip hop and ballet, techno and classical music. The visual work not only sets the scene, but also expands the narrative, punctuating rhythms, atmospheres and emotional states in constant change.
coppelia.p3: The project was developed in the context of a collaboration between the Degree in Digital Arts and Multimedia and the Matosinhos Dance Academy, culminating in a presentation at Sala Suggia at Casa da Música, on June 29, 2024.
coppelia.credits:
Visual Team: Diana Coelho, Diogo Tavares, Enzo Morellato, Joana Pinto, João Silva
Guidance: Tiago Campeã
Artistic Direction: Diana Amaral

today.subtitle: under development
today.p1: In a cramped, lamplit cafe on a winding street, three old men stuff themselves and dance as if fleeing death itself. A fortune-teller appears to seal their fate, and they set off into the thick, dark night. With toy pistols in their pockets and a fever in their bones. Their quest leads them to a house ablaze, which they enter one by one, with silent reverence, forever transformed by the flames.
today.p2: Directed by Michał Wilk, this film is about men who have outgrown their own sense of wonder - and about a night when they rediscover it. The story moves from the theatricality of the banquet to the silence of the fire: a ritual of transformation, performed with unflinching ferocity. The project is currently in pre-production, with filming scheduled for May 2026.
